```json
[
  "Machine learning is a foundational AI technology that enables computers and systems to learn from data, make predictions, classify information, and make decisions without explicit programming, often by replicating human learning processes.",
  "Machine learning is broadly categorized into supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning, with semi-supervised learning combining aspects of the first two.",
  "Supervised learning trains models on labeled data to map inputs to target outputs.",
  "Common supervised learning algorithms include linear regression, neural networks, support vector machines, and decision trees/random forests, which are applied in areas like predictive analytics, sentiment analysis, and object or image recognition.",
  "Unsupervised learning trains algorithms on unlabeled data to independently identify patterns and structures, creating their own labels.",
  "Common unsupervised learning algorithms include principal component analysis, K-means clustering, and probabilistic clustering, used for anomaly detection, customer segmentation, and powering recommendation engines.",
  "Reinforcement learning, a type of unsupervised learning, enables machines to learn through trial and error by making decisions and using a gamified reward system, with applications in market personalization, optimization, and financial predictions.",
  "Deep learning is a type of machine learning that specifically refers to neural networks utilizing many layers, often called deep neural networks."
]
```
json_data: Machine learning is a foundational AI technology that enables computers and systems to learn from data, make predictions, classify information, and make decisions without explicit programming, often by replicating human learning processes.
Machine learning is broadly categorized into supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning, with semi-supervised learning combining aspects of the first two.
Supervised learning trains models on labeled data to map inputs to target outputs.

Common supervised learning algorithms include linear regression, neural networks, support vector machines, and decision trees/random forests, which are applied in areas like predictive analytics, sentiment analysis, and object or image recognition.
Unsupervised learning trains algorithms on unlabeled data to independently identify patterns and structures, creating their own labels.

Common unsupervised learning algorithms include principal component analysis, K-means clustering, and probabilistic clustering, used for anomaly detection, customer segmentation, and powering recommendation engines.

Reinforcement learning, a type of unsupervised learning, enables machines to learn through trial and error by making decisions and using a gamified reward system, with applications in market personalization, optimization, and financial predictions.
Deep learning is a type of machine learning that specifically refers to neural networks utilizing many layers, often called deep neural networks.
